task_description:
You are an OS engineer comparing `jj` to Git behavior. You have a colocated Git and jj repository at `/home/user/project`.

Your task is to:
1. Create a new commit in jj with the description `Add feature A` that adds a file `feature_a.txt` with the content `Feature A`.
2. Create a jj bookmark named `feature-a` pointing to this new commit.
3. Export the jj bookmarks to Git so that Git is aware of the `feature-a` branch.
4. Using `git`, create a new commit on the `feature-a` branch that adds a file `feature_b.txt` with the content `Feature B`, with the description `Add feature B`.
5. Import the changes from Git back into jj, so that jj recognizes the new commit and updates the `feature-a` bookmark.
6. In jj, create a new commit on top of `feature-a` that adds `feature_c.txt` with the content `Feature C`, with the description `Add feature C`.
7. Run `jj git export` to push this final state back to Git.
8. Write the commit hash of the final `feature-a` bookmark in jj to a file `/home/user/final_hash.txt`.

Make sure that by the end, both Git and jj show the `feature-a` branch pointing to the commit containing `feature_c.txt`.
truth:
Initial state:
A colocated jj and git repo at `/home/user/project`.
`git init` and `jj git init --colocate` have been run.

Final state:
The repo has a bookmark `feature-a` pointing to a commit with description `Add feature C`.
The Git repo also has a branch `feature-a` pointing to the exact same commit.
The files `feature_a.txt`, `feature_b.txt`, and `feature_c.txt` exist in the history of `feature-a`.
`/home/user/final_hash.txt` contains the jj commit ID of the `feature-a` bookmark.
